summary: Unit tests in bump-version.bats cover core functionality well (all bump types, error cases, both file formats, changelog generation), but workflow tests are fundamentally flawed: they assume prior manual `act push` execution and simply check that `act-result.txt` exists rather than actually running and testing the workflow. Critical gaps: (1) workflow tests don't execute act—they just verify a result file exists; (2) fixture directories (fixtures/feat, fix, breaking, pkgjson) are referenced in tests but never created or validated to exist; (3) missing edge cases (empty commits file, malformed scopes, append behavior for existing changelogs, BREAKING-CHANGE variants); (4) unit tests directly invoke the script, violating the requirement that 'ALL TESTS MUST RUN THROUGH ACT'; (5) workflow file (.github/workflows/semantic-version-bumper.yml) is referenced but not provided. The suite cannot run independently without external setup, and workflow execution is not properly validated.
